title: Geology Honours

prerequisites.text: A BSc degree with a major in Geology; first qualifying courses in Chemistry and Mathematics. A first qualifying course in Physics is recommended. Registrations are limited to 16; acceptance at the discretion of the Head of Department based on quality of final year results, material covered in undergraduate curriculum and referee reports. Preference given to UCT graduates.

outline: Students are required to take 7 compulsory modules covering: Geochemistry (including Isotope and Marine Geochemistry), Geophysics, Economic Geology, Igneous, Metamorphic and Mantle Petrology, Palaeontology, Quaternary Geology, Petroleum Geology, Sedimentary Basins, Tectonics, Geological Data Interpretation & Analysis, and Scientific Communication. In addition, each student undertakes a supervised research project (approved by the Honours course co-ordinator and Head of Department). All students are required to attend a two-week fieldtrip held during the year.
assessment: Modules are examined mid-year and in October (the latter includes a 3-hour General Exam). Examinations count 45%; practical and assignment work done during the year counts 30%; research project counts 25%. Sub-minima required: 40% for overall examination mark and 50% for the research project. Coursework and project codes must each be passed separately for the award of the degree.
